text: The surgeon performs medial collateral mobilization, followed by high ligation of the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) and vein (IMV). The total mesorectal excision begins at the posterior avascular plane, progressing to free the lateral aspects and finally mobilizing the anterior side until reaching the pelvic floor muscles.

The surgeon transects the sigmoid colon using an Echelon stapler. The surgical wounds are closed, and an end colostomy is formed. The patient is repositioned into a prone jackknife position for the perineal procedure, where the anus is closed with a purse-string suture.

The surgeon performs a skin incision and reaches the abdominal dissection plane at the posterior side. The lateral sides are dissected, and the specimen is retrieved. The rectum is mobilized from the vagina, and the wound is closed with interrupted 3-0 Vicryl sutures. The complete total mesorectal excision (TME) specimen is visible.
